pattern: Each downward step doubles the accumulated value and adds the new bit, so a single DFS that carries the running number sums the leaves' numbers without rebuilding any string.
hints: Reading a binary number from most significant bit means every time you descend you shift the current value left by one (multiply by two) and add the child's bit.
Carry the partial value into the recursion. When you reach a leaf, that partial value IS the number for this path.
Sum the values returned from leaves only; internal nodes just pass their accumulated value down to both children.

edge_inputs.description: leading zeros along a path still count positionally
one-sided chain forms a single long number

stress.description: random tree of 0/1 bits via level-order array with nulls
stress.note: values restricted to 0 and 1 per constraints
